content:
First, observe that the 3×3 grid is a bipartite graph (checker‐board coloring) with one color‐class of size 5 and the other of size 4.  An “Isthmian’’ labeling requires every edge to join an odd to an even, so all 5 black squares must carry the 5 odd numbers {1,3,5,7,9} and the 4 white squares the 4 even numbers {2,4,6,8}.  There is no other choice of parity–pattern (we can’t swap, since then we’d need 5 evens and 4 odds).

Once the parity‐pattern is fixed, we can permute the 5 odds among the 5 black squares in 5! ways and the 4 evens among the 4 white squares in 4! ways.  Hence the total number of raw Isthmian labelings is

 5!·4! = 120·24 = 2880.

We now quotient out by the four rotations of the square.  By Burnside’s Lemma the number of orbits is

\[
\frac{1}{4}\bigl(\underbrace{2880}_{\text{id}}+\underbrace{0}_{90°}+\underbrace{0}_{180°}+\underbrace{0}_{270°}\bigr)
= \frac{2880}{4}=720.
\]

(The only labeling fixed by a nontrivial rotation would have to repeat numbers, which is impossible.)

Therefore the number of distinct Isthmian arrangements up to rotation is 

\boxed{720}.
